L1.area: Command-line tools and CLI execution logic

L2.description: A nil pointer dereference vulnerability exists where the application's output writer is used without being properly initialized, causing a segmentation fault when processing crafted bytecode inputs.

L3.area: EVM bytecode execution command-line tool and its output formatting logic
L3.description: A nil pointer dereference vulnerability exists where the EVM command-line tool's output writer can be nil when processing and displaying results, causing a segmentation fault (SEGV) when attacker-crafted bytecode is executed through the command-line interface.

ground_truth.reason: The runCmd function in the EVM command-line tool uses ctx.App.ErrWriter and ctx.App.Writer for output formatting, but these writers can be nil, causing a nil pointer dereference (SEGV) when crafted bytecode triggers error or output paths.
